summary: Partially decompiled camera initialization and several small camera accessors/mode helpers in src/melee/cm/camera.c, replacing placeholder nonmatching markers for Camera_80028B9C, Camera_8002FE38, and Camera_800307AC. The PR also tightened camera.h prototypes for two newly typed functions. The main contribution was establishing initialization behavior for global camera state cm_80452C68 and CameraBox subject allocation/linking.

key_files.role: Implemented partial camera initialization and small camera helper functions; added required memory and lb includes.
Updated prototypes for Camera_8002FE38 and Camera_800307AC from unknown signatures to typed void/Vec forms.

what_changed: Added includes for baselib/memory.h and lb/lb_00B0.h in camera.c, supporting use of HSD_MemAlloc/memzero and related declarations.

Implemented Camera_80028B9C(int n_subjects), initializing movement, movement_lerp, FOV, background color fields, near/far planes, mode, bitfield-like flags, debug mode last_mode, gobj, and camera subject lists.

Camera_80028B9C now allocates n_subjects CameraBox entries with HSD_MemAlloc, stores the allocation in cm_804D6458 and cm_804D645C, zeroes it, and links entries through their prev field with a NULL terminator.

Implemented Camera_8002FE38(void) as a partial match that sets cm_80452C68.mode to 6 and clears cm_80452C68.unk_341_b1; additional likely behavior remains present only as commented-out exploratory code.
Implemented Camera_800307AC(Vec* arg0) as a simple getter copying cm_80452C68.movement.interest into the output Vec.
Updated camera.h signatures for Camera_8002FE38 to void Camera_8002FE38(void) and Camera_800307AC to void Camera_800307AC(Vec*).
Removed several commented-out experimental lines inside Camera_80028F5C, reducing noise around vector copy and bitfield/default-size initialization.
smart_moves: Started Camera_80028B9C by aliasing interest_pos and eye_pos to existing cm_803BCB64 camera vectors, then reused full Vec assignment to populate both current and target movement fields. This avoids decomposing vector copies into component writes unless required for matching.
Mirrored initialization between movement and movement_lerp, preserving the apparent intent that both camera interpolation states begin from the same interest, position, and FOV.
Used memzero for a large contiguous unknown region and for the newly allocated CameraBox array, a practical tactic when struct subfields are not fully understood but the binary clearly clears memory.
Kept unresolved Camera_8002FE38 behavior as comments rather than claiming a full implementation, making the partial-match status explicit.
Improved header prototypes only where implementation evidence existed, avoiding broader speculative signature changes.
decomp_lessons: For large global state initializers, partial matches can still be valuable: establish verified scalar/vector assignments, then use memzero for opaque contiguous regions until the struct layout is better understood.
When multiple fields are initialized from the same source Vec, direct struct assignment can both clarify intent and help match compiler-generated copy patterns.
Camera subject pools appear to be allocated as a contiguous CameraBox array and linked into a free-list-like chain through CameraBox.prev, with the final entry terminated by NULL.
Header cleanup should accompany decompiled functions once signatures are known; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete void and pointer types improves future callsite work.
Leaving commented candidate writes in a partial function can document suspected remaining behavior, but future follow-up should either match them properly or remove them to avoid stale noise.
naming_conventions: Camera functions retain address-based names such as Camera_80028B9C, Camera_8002FE38, and Camera_800307AC until semantic names are established.
Global camera symbols use cm_ prefixes and address-based names, including cm_80452C68, cm_803BCB64, cm_804D6458, cm_804D645C, cm_804D6460, cm_804D6464, and cm_804D6468.
Unknown or partially understood fields remain offset/bitfield-style, such as unk_8C, unk_AC, unk_2bc, unk_2c0, unk_398_b0, unk_399_b6, unk_39A_b7, and unk_341_b1.
Temporary local names such as cam_box, interest_pos, and eye_pos were chosen to expose apparent camera semantics without over-renaming global or struct fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used full Vec assignment for position/interest copies rather than manual x/y/z component assignment.
Used memzero(cm_80452C68.unk_8C, 0x224) for a large cleared region in the global camera state.
Used memzero(&cm_80452C68 + 0x380, 0x18), an address-arithmetic clear that suggests matching constraints or an incomplete struct layout; this should be revisited once the struct is better typed.
Allocated CameraBox storage with HSD_MemAlloc(n_subjects * sizeof(CameraBox)) and immediately zeroed the allocation, matching common pool initialization patterns.
Built the CameraBox chain with a simple for loop assigning cm_804D645C[i].prev = &cm_804D645C[i + 1], then setting the last prev to NULL.
Converted simple unknown placeholders into exact small helpers: Camera_800307AC is a direct output-parameter copy of movement.interest.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No issue comment body text was included in the provided context, so reviewer intent or rationale beyond the title and diff cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching Camera_80028B9C to see whether the partial initializer became a full match or whether memzero(&cm_80452C68 + 0x380, 0x18) was replaced with typed fields.
Search for later struct updates to cm_80452C68 fields around offsets 0x341, 0x380, 0x398, 0x399, and 0x39A.
Look for follow-up work on Camera_8002FE38 to determine whether the commented target_interest/target_position/FOV assignments were validated.
Check callsites of Camera_800307AC for whether the Vec* output signature enabled additional matching or type cleanup.
Compare CameraBox list operations in other functions to confirm whether prev is serving as a free-list next pointer during initialization.
